rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use type scopes for output dependency guarantees.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all expressions have valid types and references to avoid '<error>' type issues.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to prevent resolution failures.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes.
Validate that scalar type names in AGREE match supported types: int, real, bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Guarantee expressions must relate visible inputs to visible outputs, not implementation-only paths.
anti_patterns: Do not add new package references or data types to AGREE annexes that are not already defined in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation-level annexes; use component type annexes for such contracts.
Do not declare constants or equations with unresolved identifiers or invalid scalar types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about empty packages
AGREE validator diagnostics about assume/guarantee syntax
